companies.name: OpenAI
companies.blurb: Ships ChatGPT agents (Operator/computer-use) plus the Agents SDK and Responses API for building autonomous, tool-using agents.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Preparedness / Safety Systems
companies.trigger: Independent external red-team attestation before shipping higher-autonomy computer-use agents; regulatory and enterprise-customer demand for third-party multi-agent safety audits.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.blurb: Builds Claude with computer-use and the Claude Agent SDK; Claude Code drives autonomous coding agents at scale.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Frontier Red Team / Alignment
companies.trigger: Pre-deployment external red-teaming of autonomous agent capabilities (Claude Code, computer-use) ahead of IPO scrutiny and frontier-safety commitments.

companies.name: Google DeepMind
companies.blurb: Gemini-powered agents (Project Mariner browser agent, Agentspace, Gemini computer-use) for autonomous web and workflow tasks.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of AGI Safety / Responsible AI
companies.trigger: Independent audit of autonomous browser/computer-use agents at consumer scale; Frontier Safety Framework and EU AI Act compliance pressure.

companies.name: Meta AI
companies.blurb: Llama frontier models plus Meta AI assistant agents embedded across its apps and an open agent tooling stack.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of AI Red Team / Responsible AI
companies.trigger: Third-party red-teaming of assistant agents acting across billions of social-app users; open-model misuse and multi-agent abuse scenarios.

companies.name: xAI
companies.blurb: Grok models with agentic tool-use and computer/browser control integrated into X and the Grok app.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Safety / VP Engineering
companies.trigger: External red-teaming of multi-agent Grok systems and computer/browser control integrated into a public social platform; reputational and content-safety exposure.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.blurb: Open-weight frontier models plus Le Chat agents and an Agents API with built-in connectors and code execution.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / VP Engineering
companies.trigger: Independent agent safety audits for EU enterprise/government customers; EU AI Act compliance and sovereign-AI trust positioning.

companies.name: Cohere
companies.blurb: Enterprise Command models tuned for agentic RAG and tool-use, deployed as North workplace agents.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Security / Trust (security-first enterprise AI)
companies.trigger: Third-party red-teaming to back security-first enterprise positioning; regulated-industry customers (banks, healthcare) demanding independent agent attestation.

companies.name: DeepSeek
companies.blurb: Open frontier reasoning models (R-series) widely adopted as the low-cost backbone for autonomous agents.
companies.buyer_persona: Research Lead / Founder
companies.trigger: Demand for independent safety evaluation as open models become the default agent backbone; misuse and jailbreak exposure flagged by downstream enterprise adopters.

companies.name: Alibaba Qwen
companies.blurb: Qwen frontier models with the Qwen-Agent framework and computer-use variants for tool-calling and GUI automation.
companies.buyer_persona: Trust & Safety Lead / Research Director
companies.trigger: Independent red-teaming of computer-use/GUI agents for enterprise and overseas customers; safety attestation to expand Qwen adoption beyond China.

companies.name: Amazon AGI (Nova)
companies.blurb: Builds Nova frontier models plus the Nova Act browser-agent SDK and Bedrock AgentCore, the production stack for deploying fleets of UI-automating agents on AWS.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Responsible AI / Director, Frontier Model Safety (AWS Agentic AI org)
companies.trigger: Enterprise/government customers running Nova Act agent fleets that touch real workflows and money demand independent red-team attestations; an autonomous-browser-agent exploit (e.g. prompt injection draining a customer workflow) would force third-party auditing.

companies.name: Microsoft AI
companies.blurb: Runs in-house MAI models alongside Copilot agents and Copilot Studio, the platform enterprises use to build and deploy autonomous agents across Microsoft 365 and business workflows.
companies.buyer_persona: Deputy CISO for AI / Head of Responsible AI
companies.trigger: Regulatory pressure (EU AI Act) and enterprise procurement demanding independent agent-safety audits; a high-profile Copilot agent incident (data exfiltration via injected content, unauthorized actions) would create urgency for external red-teaming.

companies.name: Reka AI
companies.blurb: Builds natively multimodal frontier models (Reka Flash/Core/Edge) and ships agentic platforms Reka Research and Reka Vision that browse, reason and act over video, docs and the web.
companies.buyer_persona: VP Engineering / Head of AI Safety
companies.trigger: Enterprise customers deploying Reka Research agents against private documents need assurance against data leakage and tool-use failures; landing a regulated-industry account would require a third-party safety audit.

companies.name: AI21 Labs
companies.blurb: Builds Jamba long-context hybrid-SSM frontier models and Maestro, an agentic planning/orchestration layer with multi-path planning and in-step validation for reliable enterprise task execution.
companies.buyer_persona: VP Engineering / Head of Enterprise AI Reliability
companies.trigger: Maestro is explicitly sold on reliability and regulatory compliance for mission-critical workflows; a regulated customer (finance/healthcare) requiring independent validation of agent decision-paths is the natural trigger.

companies.name: Moonshot AI (Kimi)
companies.blurb: Builds the open-weight Kimi K2 frontier models with strong long-context agentic tool-use and a sub-agent 'Agent Swarm' architecture powering autonomous coding and research agents.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Model Safety / founder (CEO Yang Zhilin)
companies.trigger: Western/enterprise adoption of open-weight Kimi agents raises supply-chain and jailbreak concerns; pressure to demonstrate safety parity with US labs (or an agentic misuse incident) would drive demand for independent red-teaming.

companies.name: Cognition (Devin)
companies.blurb: Builds Devin, an autonomous AI software engineer that plans, writes, tests and ships code end-to-end across enterprise codebases.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / VP Engineering (and Head of Security)
companies.trigger: Devin autonomously committing to production raises risk of insecure or sabotaged code; enterprise security review or a public agent-vuln incident drives third-party red-teaming.

companies.name: Cursor (Anysphere)
companies.blurb: Builds the Cursor AI coding IDE with an autonomous agent mode for multi-file edits, background tasks and end-to-end task completion.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / CTO
companies.trigger: Agent mode making autonomous multi-file changes and running commands creates prompt-injection and unsafe-execution risk; enterprise procurement or a viral agent-failure incident triggers external red-teaming.

companies.name: Replit
companies.blurb: Builds Replit Agent, which generates, builds and deploys full applications from natural-language prompts in a cloud dev environment.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / VP Engineering
companies.trigger: Replit Agent autonomously deploying user apps (plus a prior incident of an agent deleting a production database) makes sandbox-escape a board-level concern; Fortune-500 expansion demands independent agent safety audits.

companies.name: Factory
companies.blurb: Builds autonomous coding 'Droids' that handle dev tasks across the SDLC, including long-horizon, always-on agents with model routing and governance.
companies.buyer_persona: VP Engineering / Head of Platform (with Security)
companies.trigger: Always-on autonomous Droids acting across the SDLC raise governance and unsafe-action risk; Factory's governance emphasis plus enterprise procurement make independent red-teaming a natural requirement.

companies.name: Poolside
companies.blurb: Builds frontier code-generation models (Malibu/Point) deployed inside customer environments to power autonomous software-engineering agents.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / founder
companies.trigger: Deploying autonomous code-writing agents inside regulated customer environments where a hijacked agent could ship vulnerable code or exfiltrate data; enterprise security reviews demand third-party red-teaming.

companies.name: Augment Code
companies.blurb: Agentic AI coding assistant tuned for large enterprise codebases via a deep context engine.
companies.buyer_persona: VP Engineering / Head of Developer Productivity
companies.trigger: Enterprises running coding agents against proprietary monorepos demand assurance the agent won't leak code, follow injected instructions, or introduce vulnerabilities; security/compliance gating before rollout.

companies.name: Magic.dev
companies.blurb: Builds frontier models with ultra-long-context ('LTM') memory aimed at fully autonomous software engineering.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / founder
companies.trigger: Moving from research to commercial release of a fully autonomous coding agent would need third-party red-teaming to validate safety before letting an agent act unsupervised on real codebases.

companies.name: Codeium / Windsurf
companies.blurb: Windsurf agentic IDE with Cascade flows that autonomously plan and make multi-file code changes; now owned by Cognition.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / VP Engineering (now under Cognition)
companies.trigger: Autonomous unsupervised multi-file edits/commits risk destructive changes, prompt-injection via repo content, or secret leakage; post-acquisition Devin integration raises blast radius.

companies.name: Tabnine
companies.blurb: Enterprise AI coding assistant evolved into an agentic platform with private/air-gapped deployment and an enterprise context engine.
companies.buyer_persona: CISO / VP Engineering
companies.trigger: Privacy-first/air-gapped regulated customers (finance, defense) require third-party audit of agent behavior before allowing agents to act in secured environments; security is the differentiator.

companies.name: Sourcegraph (Amp/Cody)
companies.blurb: Code-aware AI agents for search, fixes, and large-scale refactors across big codebases (Cody Enterprise; Amp now a separate company).
companies.buyer_persona: VP Engineering / Platform Engineering lead
companies.trigger: Agents performing large-scale autonomous refactors across monorepos risk cascading breakage or acting on injected instructions; enterprise buyers gate adoption on independent safety review.

companies.name: Zencoder
companies.blurb: Repo-aware agentic coding assistant (by For Good AI) that autonomously executes multi-step dev tasks with self-correcting agents.
companies.buyer_persona: VP Engineering / founder
companies.trigger: Autonomous task-executing agents acting on repos need validation against hijacking and destructive actions; a smaller vendor uses third-party red-teaming as a trust signal to win enterprise deals.

companies.name: Lovable
companies.blurb: AI agent that builds and deploys full-stack web apps from natural-language prompts ('vibe coding').
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / CTO
companies.trigger: Untrusted end users prompting an agent to generate and deploy live apps creates abuse surface (malicious apps, prompt injection, insecure code at scale); a public incident or compliance pressure drives third-party red-teaming.

companies.name: Bolt (StackBlitz)
companies.blurb: In-browser AI agent that generates and deploys full-stack apps, running Node.js client-side via WebContainers.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / CTO / founder
companies.trigger: An agent generating and deploying full-stack apps for untrusted users at scale risks abuse and insecure code; in-browser WebContainer execution adds sandbox-escape concerns motivating red-teaming.

companies.name: Sierra
companies.blurb: Conversational AI agents that handle customer experience and support end-to-end for enterprises.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of AI / CTO
companies.trigger: Fortune-50 customers demand independent assurance that autonomous agents won't hallucinate, leak data, or take wrong real-world actions before signing/expanding contracts.

companies.name: Decagon
companies.blurb: AI customer-support agents that autonomously resolve tickets across chat and voice for enterprises.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of AI / VP Engineering
companies.trigger: Scaling autonomous resolution across regulated/high-volume enterprises where a single wrong agent action (refund, account change) costs money or triggers compliance exposure.

companies.name: Cresta
companies.blurb: AI agents plus real-time agent-assist for contact centers, unifying human and AI agents.
companies.buyer_persona: VP Contact Center Operations / Head of AI
companies.trigger: Real-time AI guidance and autonomous agents touching live customer conversations create risk of bad scripts/compliance violations at scale; enterprise buyers want third-party validation.

companies.name: Parloa
companies.blurb: AI Agent Management Platform for voice and chat customer service at enterprise scale.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of AI / VP Customer Service
companies.trigger: Voice agents handling regulated industries (insurance, travel) at Fortune 200 scale; reliability failures or compliance breaches push buyers toward independent red-teaming.

companies.name: Forethought
companies.blurb: Multi-agent, omnichannel generative AI that autonomously resolves customer-support tickets.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / Head of Support AI
companies.trigger: Multi-agent omnichannel architecture handling massive interaction volume raises surface area for cascading agent failures; enterprise buyers want adversarial testing of the orchestration.

companies.name: Ada
companies.blurb: AI customer-service agents automating support resolution across chat, email, and voice channels.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Automated CX / VP Engineering
companies.trigger: Brand-facing agents automating resolution across channels risk reputational hallucinations; enterprise security/CX leaders seek third-party safety audits before scaling deflection.

companies.name: Intercom (Fin)
companies.blurb: Fin AI agent autonomously resolves customer-support conversations across thousands of customers.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / Head of AI
companies.trigger: Fin operates at massive scale where even a low error rate produces many bad outcomes; published resolution metrics invite scrutiny and create demand for independent agent red-teaming.

companies.name: Crescendo
companies.blurb: AI-plus-human customer experience platform with autonomous support agents and human-in-the-loop.
companies.buyer_persona: VP Customer Experience / Head of AI
companies.trigger: Blended AI+human CX model where autonomous agents front customer conversations; scaling deflection raises stakes on agent accuracy and pushes need for external validation.

companies.name: Lorikeet
companies.blurb: AI support agents that handle complex, high-stakes customer workflows in regulated fintech/healthtech.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / Compliance Lead
companies.trigger: Agents executing high-stakes actions in regulated fintech/healthtech (money movement, medical triage) where errors carry legal/compliance liability — the strongest case for mandatory third-party red-teaming.

companies.name: Gradient Labs
companies.blurb: Autonomous AI agents that run customer-operations workflows (disputes, KYC, fraud, complaints) for regulated financial institutions.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Compliance / Chief Risk Officer
companies.trigger: A regulator or banking customer demands independent assurance that autonomous agents handling money, KYC, and disputes won't breach consumer-protection rules.

companies.name: Maven AGI
companies.blurb: Enterprise CX platform deploying generative AI agents that autonomously resolve customer-support inquiries.
companies.buyer_persona: VP of Customer Experience / Head of Trust & Safety
companies.trigger: An enterprise rollout where agents autonomously answer customers at scale creates reputational/compliance risk that demands third-party red-teaming before expansion.

companies.name: PolyAI
companies.blurb: Enterprise voice AI agents (built on its own LLMs) that handle customer-service call traffic for large brands.
companies.buyer_persona: VP of Customer Service / Contact Center / Head of CX
companies.trigger: A regulated enterprise client (banking, utilities) requires proof that voice agents won't mishandle PII, give bad financial advice, or be socially engineered.

companies.name: Retell AI
companies.blurb: Platform for building, deploying, and managing production voice agents for call automation.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / founder (developer-led platform)
companies.trigger: Customers deploying Retell voice agents at scale push liability upstream, requiring the platform to demonstrate adversarial robustness and guardrails.

companies.name: Vapi
companies.blurb: Developer platform for building and orchestrating production voice AI agents.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / VP Engineering / founder
companies.trigger: Enterprise customers demand assurance that agents built on the platform resist prompt injection and social engineering.

companies.name: Bland AI
companies.blurb: Enterprise platform for programmable phone-calling voice agents handling complex, high-stakes conversations at scale.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / CTO
companies.trigger: High-stakes outbound/inbound calls (insurance, finance) plus past press scrutiny over agent guardrails create demand for independent adversarial audits.

companies.name: ElevenLabs
companies.blurb: Voice model maker whose ElevenAgents platform lets enterprises deploy voice and chat conversational agents at scale.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of AI Safety / Chief Trust Officer
companies.trigger: Voice-cloning misuse risk, IPO-readiness scrutiny, and enterprise agent deployments at massive scale demand external red-teaming and safety attestations.

companies.name: Phonely
companies.blurb: Voice AI agents that answer business phone calls, qualify leads, and book appointments.
companies.buyer_persona: Founder / Head of Operations
companies.trigger: Scaling autonomous phone agents into regulated verticals requires proof the agents won't be jailbroken or mishandle sensitive caller data.

companies.name: Lindy
companies.blurb: No-code platform for building AI agents that automate business workflows (support, sales, email) across thousands of integrations.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Trust & Safety / founder
companies.trigger: Customer-facing agents take real actions across thousands of workflows; a prompt-injection or wrong-action incident would push them toward third-party red-teaming before enterprise/regulated deals.

companies.name: /dev/agents
companies.blurb: Building a cloud operating system and developer platform for general-purpose AI agents that coordinate across a user's devices.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / co-founder (David Singleton)
companies.trigger: As an agent OS that other developers build on, a platform-level safety or sandbox-escape failure would propagate to every app on it; they'd want third-party audits to certify the platform before broad adoption.

companies.name: Adept
companies.blurb: Built action-taking agents that operate software via UI; most founders/team (incl. CEO David Luan) joined Amazon's AGI lab in 2024, leaving a smaller team focused on enabling agentic AI.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Engineering / CEO (Zach Brock)
companies.trigger: Action-taking agents that click through real software carry high blast radius; if Adept ships an enabling-layer product, customers would demand third-party safety validation.

companies.name: CrewAI
companies.blurb: Open-source and enterprise multi-agent orchestration platform for building and running production agent 'crews'.
companies.buyer_persona: VP Engineering / CTO
companies.trigger: As the orchestration layer beneath Fortune-500 multi-agent deployments, a coordination failure or injected sub-agent could cascade; enterprise buyers require independent red-teaming of agent crews.

companies.name: Tektonic AI
companies.blurb: Agentic AI that automates enterprise revenue operations and back-office workflows (sales, deal-desk, RevOps).
companies.buyer_persona: VP RevOps / CISO
companies.trigger: Agents that autonomously make revenue decisions and act on CRM/financial systems handle money and sensitive data; an erroneous or manipulated action pushes enterprise customers to demand third-party safety audits.

companies.name: MultiOn
companies.blurb: Web-browsing agents that take real actions across websites (booking, planning, automation) exposed via an Agent API.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / founder (Div Garg)
companies.trigger: Agents taking actions across arbitrary live websites (logins, purchases) are prime prompt-injection targets; partners and API customers would require third-party red-teaming before trusting at scale.

companies.name: Induced AI
companies.blurb: Browser-automation agents that turn plain-English instructions into Chromium-driven back-office workflows.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / co-founder
companies.trigger: Back-office agents operating on internal portals and customer data carry compliance risk; enterprise customers want independent safety audits before granting credentialed access.

companies.name: Cognosys
companies.blurb: Autonomous web/workflow agents for knowledge work (research, reports, scheduled automations); acquired by Cohere in May 2025 and folded into the North product.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of AI / CTO (now within Cohere)
companies.trigger: Knowledge-work agents producing research/reports inside an enterprise product (Cohere North) would need red-teaming for hallucination, data-exfiltration, and injection risk before regulated rollout.

companies.name: Relevance AI
companies.blurb: No-code 'AI workforce' operating system for building and managing teams of specialized AI agents that collaborate like employees.
companies.buyer_persona: Head of Operations / CISO
companies.trigger: Customers build autonomous agent 'employees' that act on real business systems; a runaway or manipulated agent in the workforce drives demand for third-party red-teaming before scaling to sensitive workflows.

companies.name: Beam AI
companies.blurb: Self-learning AI agents that automate enterprise back-office processes (finance, HR, customer support, ops).
companies.buyer_persona: VP of Operations / Head of Automation (or CTO at smaller orgs)
companies.trigger: Production agents touching finance/HR data and customer comms create error and compliance exposure; an enterprise customer or auditor demands proof the agents won't take harmful actions.

companies.name: Harvey
companies.blurb: Domain-specific AI agents for legal and professional-services work used by big law firms and enterprise legal teams.
companies.buyer_persona: Chief Innovation Officer / General Counsel or Head of Trust & Safety
companies.trigger: Agents drafting legal work product at 1,300+ orgs; a hallucinated citation or privileged-data leak surfaces, and clients/regulators demand independent red-team assurance.

companies.name: Hebbia
companies.blurb: Matrix — an agentic data-grid that runs end-to-end research/analysis agents over documents for finance and knowledge work.
companies.buyer_persona: CISO / Head of AI at the customer, or Hebbia's own VP Eng
companies.trigger: Agents autonomously decompose and synthesize sensitive financial documents; a wrong analysis or data-segregation failure at a bank client triggers demand for third-party agent audit.

companies.name: Glean
companies.blurb: Enterprise search plus horizontal AI agents (Glean Agents) that act over company knowledge and apps at scale.
companies.buyer_persona: CISO / Head of Trust & Safety (enterprise) or Glean's VP of Security
companies.trigger: Agents with broad cross-app permissions executing 100M+ actions create a huge attack surface for prompt injection and data exfiltration; a security incident or buyer security review forces independent red-teaming.

companies.name: Sana
companies.blurb: Enterprise AI assistant and agents that work over company knowledge for learning, search, and workflow automation.
companies.buyer_persona: CISO / Head of Trust & Safety (now within Workday's security org)
companies.trigger: Acquisition by Workday means Sana agents touch core HR/finance systems; Workday's enterprise customers and security org demand audited assurance before broad agent rollout.

companies.name: EvenUp
companies.blurb: AI agents (Claims Intelligence Platform, Piai model) that automate personal-injury case prep — demand letters, medical-record analysis.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / Head of Product (or General Counsel)
companies.trigger: Agents generating settlement-driving demand letters from medical records; a fabricated medical fact or biased valuation produces malpractice/liability risk and pushes for independent agent validation.

companies.name: Eve
companies.blurb: AI platform with reasoning agents for plaintiff law firms — intake to resolution: document review, case strategy, drafting.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / Head of Engineering (or founder)
companies.trigger: Agents autonomously synthesize case strategy and draft arguments across thousands of docs; an error affecting a live case pushes demand for third-party red-teaming of agent reasoning.

companies.name: Norm Ai
companies.blurb: Compliance AI agents that convert regulations and corporate policies into executable code to check business activities.
companies.buyer_persona: Chief Compliance Officer / Chief Risk Officer
companies.trigger: Compliance agents making automated regulatory determinations for banks/insurers; a regulator or internal audit questions whether the agents themselves are reliable and unbiased, driving demand for independent validation.

companies.name: Rogo
companies.blurb: Agentic AI platform (Felix) for finance — autonomous deal screening, CIM generation, diligence, and analyst-grade research.
companies.buyer_persona: CISO / Head of AI Governance (bank), or Rogo's VP Eng
companies.trigger: Autonomous agents running diligence and generating deal materials with real money implications; a flawed output or data-room leak at a banking client forces independent red-team assurance.

companies.name: Tessl
companies.blurb: AI-native software development platform where coding agents work from human-authored specs rather than raw prompts.
companies.buyer_persona: CTO / founder
companies.trigger: Shipping autonomous coding agents into enterprise codebases raises liability for regressions and unsafe code; needs third-party audit of agent behavior to win enterprise trust.

companies.name: 11x
companies.blurb: Autonomous AI digital workers (Alice SDR, Julian voice agent) that run end-to-end sales outreach and lead qualification.
